mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate profit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate exceeds the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal consumption expenditures and a decrease in the consumer price index indicates strong real demand growth without inflationary pressure, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when applied outside post-shock recovery regimes or during steady-state expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate robust construction sector activity and consumer confidence in long-term wealth, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no significant positive correlation with housing starts levels across multiple economic cycles in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 315.0, it reflects entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where the consumer price index exceeds the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates signals a strengthening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in multivariate regression models predicting forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During recession onset regimes, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP confirms economic contraction that triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if forward returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters following a negative real GDP growth print during recession onset periods.
